title: Child Stunting Hotspot Identification In Sub-Saharan Africa
summary: Build a verified workflow for identifying where child stunting rates are highest and most persistent in Sub-Saharan Africa, linking nutrition survey data, food security indicators, and intervention coverage to support stunting reduction targeting.
why_it_matters: 149 million children under five are stunted globally — 40 percent in Sub-Saharan Africa. Stunting impairs cognitive development, educational attainment, and lifetime earnings irreversibly. Despite decades of nutrition programming, stunting rates exceed 30 percent in many districts. Knowing where stunting persists and which interventions reach those children is basic targeting. But sub-national data in the highest-burden countries is often five years old and survey-to-survey comparison is unreliable.

success_metrics.target: Stunting estimates include survey year, sample size, and comparison methodology, and identify districts with data older than five years.
success_metrics.validation_method: Run schema validation and reviewer spot-checks.

success_metrics.target: Hotspot classification requires at least two survey rounds and rules out random survey variation.
No nutrition program recommendation is merged without review and replication.
success_metrics.validation_method: Check task status, review records, and safety flags.

review_policy.merge_gate: Schema validation, domain review, and replication for quantitative analyses.
